slides.title: Scaling the agent platform to 10× revenue
slides.subtitle: Three bets, one constraint, and a 90-day path to sign-off.
slides.author: ZimaBlueAI · Strategy
slides.notes: Welcome. Today we'll cover three strategic bets, their tradeoffs, and the path to commercial readiness by end of Q3.

slides.title: Six items, 25 minutes
items: Risks and what could go wrong
slides.notes: We'll spend most time on bets and the capital allocation tradeoff. Architecture and risk are reference, not debate.

slides.title: State of the platform
slides.subtitle: What's working, what's flat, what's leaking
slides.notes: The platform is healthy on adoption but flat on ARPU. We dig into why next.

slides.title: Adoption up, ARPU flat, churn under control
slides.notes: Adoption is the headline. ARPU is the problem we'll solve in the next section.

slides.title: The three bets
slides.subtitle: Pick one. Resource it for real.

slides.title: Three doors, one budget
slides.lead: Each bet is real and defensible. The question is not which is best in isolation — it's which one we can resource to actually win.

bullets.note: highest ARPU lift but slowest GTM cycle

bullets.text: Bet C — Platform play (open SDK + marketplace)
bullets.note: largest TAM, also largest engineering bill

slides.notes: I'll argue for Bet A. Holding scope on B, deferring C. Reasons next slide.

slides.title: Bet A vs Bet C — the real tradeoff

left.heading: Bet A · Vertical depth
items: Eng team already has domain context
Risk: regulatory review on legal vertical

right.heading: Bet C · Platform play
items: Marketplace ops is a new muscle

slides.notes: Bet A returns capital in 2 quarters; Bet C takes 4. We are not yet at scale to absorb Bet C's J-curve.

slides.quote: We didn't lose because we picked the wrong bet — we lost because we hedged across two and couldn't fund either.
slides.author: Board chair, last cycle

slides.title: Decision: ship Bet A
slides.subtitle: Hold Bet B. Reassess Bet C in Q1 2027.
slides.notes: Asking for board sign-off on Bet A budget reallocation and explicit deferral of Bet C investment until Q1.
